metadata.title: Enumerate Backdoor Paths and Find an Adjustment Set with Claude
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration: Six backdoor paths hide in a pricing DAG. Claude finds them all in seconds, then proves one adjustment set closes every path.
beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear.

beats.narration: The backdoor criterion: block every non-causal path from Treatment to Outcome that enters T through a back-arrow. Find the right set to condition on — and only that set.
We give Claude a six-node pricing DAG as an edge list and ask it to enumerate every backdoor path from ad-spend to sales.

props.command:
claude "Given this DAG:
  Brand→T, Brand→Y
  Comp→T, Comp→Y
  Quality→T, Quality→Y
  T→Y
enumerate every backdoor path T→Y,
then propose a valid adjustment set."

beats.narration: Claude finds three backdoor paths: T-back-Brand-Y, T-back-Comp-Y, and T-back-Quality-Y. The adjustment set is Brand, Comp, Quality — conditioning on all three closes every path.

props.code:
# backdoor_paths.py — Claude Code output
edges = [('Brand','T'),('Brand','Y'),
         ('Comp','T'), ('Comp','Y'),
         ('Quality','T'),('Quality','Y'),
         ('T','Y')]

# Backdoor paths from T to Y:
# 1. T ← Brand → Y
# 2. T ← Comp  → Y
# 3. T ← Quality → Y

# Adjustment set: {Brand, Comp, Quality}
# Verify: each path has a node in the set
for path, blocker in [
    ('T←Brand→Y','Brand'),
    ('T←Comp→Y', 'Comp'),
    ('T←Quality→Y','Quality')]:
    print(f'{path}  blocked by {blocker} ✓')

beats.narration: The three backdoor paths listed, each with its structure. Below: the adjustment set in a gold box. Each path has exactly one node from the set — and that node blocks it.
We add an edge to the DAG — Comp now also influences Brand — and ask Claude to re-enumerate. Does the adjustment set need to grow?

props.command: claude "Add edge: Comp→Brand.\nRe-enumerate backdoor paths.\nDoes the adjustment set change?"

beats.narration: The new path appears: T-back-Brand-back-Comp-Y. The adjustment set already contained both Brand and Comp, so it still blocks all paths — no change needed.

The backdoor criterion turns causal identification into a graph-theory problem. Find every non-causal path from T to Y, then choose a set of nodes that blocks them all without conditioning on a collider.
Next: the E-value — how weak does an unmeasured confounder need to be to explain away your result?
Like and subscribe for more causal reasoning with Claude.
